channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate shakeup: How a secret ballot could undermine a potential Trump endorsement in race to the top
item.description: Former President Trump has yet to endorse in the Senate GOP leader race, and doing so could have risks for him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Tampa Bay counties require vote-by-mail postage
item.description: Early voting is underway this week, and supervisors of elections also expect an increase in the return of vote-by-mail ballots.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Top 5 ways education would look different under Harris vs. Trump
item.description: Education issues have flown under the radar this election cycle despite both major candidates having big ideas about how the K-12 and college systems should work.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Harris campaign abandons Biden in final weeks before Election Day: report
item.description: President Biden and Vice President Harris reportedly have no plans to appear together on the campaign trail in the final weeks before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In part of swing state Michigan, neighbors skip presidential signs in a heated campaign season
item.description: BLOOMFIELD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — The lawn of Nick Hannawa's suburban Detroit home is lined with political signs backing candidates for prosecutor, supervisor and local trustee. But Hannawa isn't promoting his presidential pick. He says he doesn't want the headaches in an polarized election year.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS and MIKE HOUSEHOLDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voting groups that got a boost from Harris' candidacy are still working to persuade voters of color
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Left-leaning voter engagement groups that saw a surge in support and energy after Vice President Kamala Harris replaced President Joe Biden as the Democrats' White House nominee are aligning with a key pillar of her campaign in the final stretch before Election Day — trying to turn
item.dc:creator: BY AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans and Democrats are both preparing for long legal battles over the 2024 election results
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Donald Trump, who still refuses to accept that he lost the 2020 election to Democrat Joe Biden, says he wants a presidential victory Nov. 5 to be so overwhelming that the results are “too big to rig.” “We want a landslide," he recently told supporters in Georgia.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, COLLEEN LONG and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Balance of power: Helene could shift political winds toward Trump, North Carolina lawmakers say
item.description: Two North Carolina officials are hopeful their state will see massive voter turnout despite the effects of Helene, and both suggested they think Donald Trump will win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats look to quash threat from Green Party's Jill Stein
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over Green Party candidate Jill Stein as they look to avoid a repeat of the 2016 presidential election, in which Stein was accused of playing spoiler in key swing states. For months, Democrats had trained their ire on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Harris says Trump is 'cruel' as she spotlights abortion restrictions in Georgia during early voting
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said Saturday that Republican former President Donald Trump was “cruel” for how he talked about the grieving family of a Georgia mother who died after waiting 20 hours for a hospital to treat her complications from an abortion pill, as she put combating re
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and CHARLOTTE KRAMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris courts voters in Atlanta with musical guest Usher
item.description: Singer Usher will also be in attendance. The vice president also campaigned earlier Saturday in Detroit alongside "Good as Hell" singer Lizzo.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Together they've been in office more than 70 years. Now their races could help swing House control
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — One is the longest-serving woman in congressional history. The other is dean of the Republican delegation from California and the chairman of a subcommittee responsible for Pentagon spending. Together, they have more than seven decades of experience serving in Congress.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris and Trump hold competing rallies in Michigan
item.description: While the two candidates have been crisscrossing the swing states for weeks, this is the first time they are literally crossing paths, with each of them holding events in the suburbs north of Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea, Asma Khalid, and Eric McDaniel, MPR News

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: Politics Friday: The 2024 election will determine who presides in Minnesota courtrooms, remember there are judges on your ballot
item.description: There are about 100 races on Minnesota’s election ballots that won’t get that much attention, and they’re all about judgeships. MPR News politics editor and his guests talk about judicial elections and why they’re different here than in other states.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Matthew Alvarez, and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Trump expresses frustration about calls to deploy Haley
item.description: “But let me just tell you: Nikki Haley and I fought, and I beat her by 50, 60, 90 points. I beat her in her own state. I beat Nikki badly.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Walter White of 'Breaking Bad' returns for New Mexico litter campaign
item.description: Two television ads featuring Bryan Cranston as Walter White, directed by "Breaking Bad" creator Vince Gilligan, were created to promote clean-up of New Mexico areas.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Salinas, Fox 5

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: How to avoid sharing election misinformation
item.description: The 2024 election season is here. As we’re waiting to learn the final results, it can be easy to be duped by false or misleading information. Here are four tips to help you avoid spreading election related misinformation.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Nguyen, MPR News

item.title: Trump focuses on women’s issues in front of all-women audience in Fox News town hall
item.description: The former president took questions on immigration, the economy and abortion in the hour-long town hall in front of a friendly crowd of women in suburban Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Canceling subscriptions has to be as easy as signing up, FTC says in a new rule
item.description: The "click-to-cancel" rule, now finalized by the Federal Trade Commission, aims to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and memberships. Companies argue the agency overstepped its authority.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh, MPR News

item.title: Control of the Minnesota House is up for grabs this fall. Here are 14 races to watch
item.description: While all seats in the Minnesota House of Representatives are on the ballot this fall, just a fraction are taking up the focus and much of the fundraising for the major parties.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: In close Minn. House races, personal problems old and new can be vexing for candidates
item.description: With the Minnesota House majority hinging on a small number of seats, flaws in candidate pasts are resurfacing as parties and groups jockey for an edge that can decide key races.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Donald Trump addresses Economic Club of Chicago
item.description: CHICAGO — Former President Donald Trump is back in Chicago Tuesday. Trump addressed the Economic Club of Chicago at Fairmont Chicago, located at 200 North Columbus Drive, ahead of the Nov. 5 election.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: Election Day Looms: Hurricane impacts on voting
item.description: Voting rules changed for impacted Florida counties after hurricane Helene, now the question is, will rules be adjusted after hurricane Milton?
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lisa Cole, Nevada Assembly District 4 candidate, discusses economic development, affordable housing, and education
item.description: Lisa Cole, a candidate for Nevada Assembly District 4, appeared on the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to discuss her platform.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: 3 voter-related lawsuits dropped in Nevada ahead of Election Day
item.description: Petitioners who filed three lawsuits challenging the maintenance of Nevada’s voter rolls have dismissed their cases, in part because voting by-mail ballot was already well underway.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
